div.p: -ffast-math is not "faster math" — it's different math. The compiler transforms your algorithm into one that's faster but semantically distinct. Only use when you understand and accept the numerical consequences.
The 1.0 gets absorbed into 1e30 due to precision limits. With -ffast-math (vectorized, reordered):
The 1.0 and -1e30 cancel first, leaving 1e30. Result: 0.0 vs 1e30 — completely different!
